summary: FluxCD infrastructure manifests implementing 5 HelmRepository sources, 4 HelmRelease operators, a self-signed ClusterIssuer, and 2 infrastructure CRs (MariaDB Galera, Memcached) under deploy/flux-system/, with a single Kustomization file producing 12 resources. HelmRepository and HelmRelease CRs are well-structured and consistent. Infrastructure CRs deviate significantly from the plan specification — MariaDB CR is missing MaxScale, has wrong storage size and name; Memcached CR is missing the image field and has wrong name.

tests_checklist.item: kustomize build produces all 12 expected resources (5 HelmRepository + 4 HelmRelease + 1 ClusterIssuer + 1 MariaDB + 1 Memcached)
All YAML files parse without errors
Every HelmRelease sourceRef.name matches a HelmRepository metadata.name
No automated tests required (declarative YAML manifests)

code_quality_checklist.item: All HelmRepository CRs follow identical structure (apiVersion, namespace, interval)
All HelmRelease CRs follow identical common settings (interval, install, upgrade)
SPDX Apache-2.0 license headers on all files (3-line format)
Kustomization resource list grouped with section comments
Documentation is comprehensive and accurate relative to implementation
No dead code or commented-out blocks

security_checklist.item: No hardcoded secrets in manifests (MariaDB root password via SecretKeyRef)
Self-signed ClusterIssuer clearly documented as development-only
No path traversal or injection risks (declarative YAML)
Namespace isolation: operators in separate namespaces (cert-manager, mariadb-system, external-secrets, memcached-system)

architecture_checklist.item: Dependency chain correct: cert-manager base, all others dependsOn cert-manager
Memcached Operator sourceRef uses c5c3-charts (shared OCI registry), not separate repo
MariaDB Galera CR matches architecture doc spec
Single Kustomization file with flat resource list for simplicity

dry_yagni_checklist.item: OpenBao HelmRepository provisioned for CC-0009 (intentional, per plan scope)

fail_fast_checklist.item: HelmRelease remediation retries set to 3 for upgrade failures
install.crds CreateReplace ensures CRDs are always present

defensive_checklist.item: Version constraints prevent unexpected major version upgrades

architecture_findings.description: Infrastructure CRs placed in deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/ instead of plan-specified deploy/infrastructure/. The plan explicitly states 'MariaDB Galera CR (deploy/infrastructure/mariadb.yaml)' and 'Memcached CR (deploy/infrastructure/memcached.yaml)' with the kustomization referencing '../infrastructure/'. The implementation nests them under deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/ instead. This is functionally equivalent but deviates from the plan without documented justification.
architecture_findings.fix: Either move files to deploy/infrastructure/ and update kustomization.yaml references to ../infrastructure/mariadb.yaml and ../infrastructure/memcached.yaml, or document the deviation rationale.

issues_found.description: MariaDB CR metadata.name is 'mariadb' but plan task 3.1 and architecture doc (03-components/01-control-plane/06-infrastructure-operators.md) specify 'openstack-db'.

MariaDB CR is missing spec.maxScale configuration. Plan task 3.1 specifies 'spec.maxScale.enabled true, spec.maxScale.replicas 2' and the review criterion explicitly requires 'MaxScale 2 replicas'. The upstream mariadb-operator CRD supports this field.
issues_found.fix:
Add to spec:
  maxScale:
    enabled: true
    replicas: 2

issues_found.description: MariaDB CR storage size is 10Gi but plan task 3.1 and architecture doc specify 100Gi. The review criterion explicitly requires '100Gi ceph-rbd'.
MariaDB CR storage is missing storageClassName. Plan task 3.1 specifies 'spec.storage.storageClassName ceph-rbd' and review criterion requires 'ceph-rbd'.
Memcached CR metadata.name is 'memcached' but plan task 3.2 and architecture doc specify 'openstack-memcached'.

Memcached CR is missing spec.image field. Plan task 3.2 specifies 'spec.image memcached:1.6'. The fake CRD at internal/common/testutil/fake_crds/memcached-operator/memcached.yaml confirms 'image' is a supported spec field (line 35-36). Note: plan-specified fields config, resources, highAvailability, and monitoring are NOT in the CRD schema and should be omitted (plan-vs-CRD discrepancy — the architecture doc describes aspirational fields not yet implemented in the operator CRD).
issues_found.fix: Add spec.image: memcached:1.6 to the Memcached CR.

issues_found.description: ClusterIssuer metadata.name is 'selfsigned-issuer' but plan task 2.1 specifies 'selfsigned-cluster-issuer'. Documentation references and future CRs may depend on this exact name.

Documentation describes MariaDB CR name as 'mariadb' and Memcached CR name as 'memcached', consistent with implementation but inconsistent with plan/architecture docs ('openstack-db' and 'openstack-memcached'). Documentation should be updated after the CRs are fixed to match the plan.
issues_found.fix: Update all name references in docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md after fixing the CR names.

suggested_improvements: Consider adding a comment to the MariaDB CR explaining that the rootPasswordSecretKeyRef secret (mariadb-root-password) will be provisioned by the ESO integration in CC-0009

The cert-manager HelmRelease version constraint was tightened to >=1.16.0 <2.0.0 (plan: >=1.0.0) and external-secrets to >=0.10.0 <1.0.0 (plan: >=0.9.0) — these are reasonable refinements but should be noted for traceability
next_steps: Fix MariaDB CR: rename to openstack-db, add maxScale (enabled: true, replicas: 2), change storage to 100Gi, add storageClassName: ceph-rbd
Fix Memcached CR: rename to openstack-memcached, add spec.image: memcached:1.6
Update docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md to reflect corrected CR names and MariaDB spec additions
Decide on infrastructure directory location: keep deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/ (current) or move to deploy/infrastructure/ (plan-specified) — document the decision

detected_patterns.name: FluxCD HelmRelease common install/upgrade settings
detected_patterns.applies_when: Adding a new HelmRelease CR for an infrastructure operator (e.g., OpenBao for CC-0009, Keystone Operator for CC-0017)
detected_patterns.description: All HelmRelease CRs in deploy/flux-system/releases/ share identical install and upgrade settings: interval 30m, install.crds CreateReplace, install.createNamespace true, upgrade.crds CreateReplace, upgrade.remediation.retries 3. All operators except cert-manager (the base layer) must include dependsOn [{name: cert-manager, namespace: cert-manager}]. The sourceRef always points to a HelmRepository in the flux-system namespace. Each operator is deployed to its own namespace (<operator>-system or <operator-name>).

examples.code:
---
apiVersion: helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v2
kind: HelmRelease
metadata:
  name: mariadb-operator
  namespace: mariadb-system
spec:
  interval: 30m
  dependsOn:
    - name: cert-manager
      namespace: cert-manager
  chart:
    spec:
      chart: mariadb-operator
      version: ">=0.30.0 <1.0.0"
      sourceRef:
        kind: HelmRepository
        name: mariadb-operator
        namespace: flux-system
  values:
    metrics:
      enabled: true
    webhook:
      enabled: true
  install:
    crds: CreateReplace
    createNamespace: true
  upgrade:
    crds: CreateReplace
    remediation:
      retries: 3

---
apiVersion: helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v2
kind: HelmRelease
metadata:
  name: memcached-operator
  namespace: memcached-system
spec:
  interval: 30m
  dependsOn:
    - name: cert-manager
      namespace: cert-manager
  chart:
    spec:
      chart: memcached-operator
      version: ">=0.1.0 <1.0.0"
      sourceRef:
        kind: HelmRepository
        name: c5c3-charts
        namespace: flux-system
  values:
    metrics:
      enabled: true
    webhook:
      enabled: true
  install:
    crds: CreateReplace
    createNamespace: true
  upgrade:
    crds: CreateReplace
    remediation:
      retries: 3
